user_query:
I’m tuning our library’s search ranking and need a small, reproducible web-scraping and config-update exercise based on the sample catalog HTML in this workspace.

Please do the following and keep all inputs/outputs under the specified paths:

1) Parse the offline catalog pages and produce datasets
- Input HTML: input/catalog_pages/*.html (three sample records).
- Write a script at scripts/extract_catalog.py (any language is fine, but make it runnable locally without network calls) that parses each page and extracts the following fields:
  - id: the last path segment of the <link rel="canonical" href="..."> URL (e.g., 1001)
  - title (text of h1.title)
  - author (text of span.author)
  - subjects (list items under ul#subjects; join with semicolons in output)
  - call_number (text of div.call-number)
  - availability (text of span.availability)
- Produce output/items.csv with header: id,title,author,subjects,call_number,availability and one row per page.
- Produce output/field_stats.json summarizing coverage with this schema:
  {
    "total_pages": <int>,
    "coverage": {
      "title": <float 0..1>,
      "author": <float 0..1>,
      "subjects": <float 0..1>,
      "call_number": <float 0..1>,
      "availability": <float 0..1>
    }
  }
  Compute coverage as the fraction of pages where the field is present and non-empty. Do not hardcode values—derive from the HTML.

2) Update the indexing configuration based on coverage
- Input config: input/indexing_config.yaml
- Rules (deterministic):
  - Set fields.title.weight to 3.0 (ensure it remains index: true).
  - Set fields.author.weight to 1.0 (ensure it remains index: true).
  - If coverage.subjects >= 0.9, set fields.subjects.weight to 1.5; otherwise leave it at 0.5. Ensure subjects remains index: true.
  - Ensure availability is present under fields with index: false and weight: 0.0 (add it if missing or update if present).
  - Add an alias so that queries for "topic" resolve to the "subjects" field (aliases.topic: subjects). Preserve any existing entries.
- Update input/indexing_config.yaml in place to reflect the above and also write the full updated file to output/indexing_config.yaml.
- Create output/config_changes.json summarizing only the changes you made in this shape:
  {
    "weights": {"title": {"before": <number>, "after": <number>}, "author": {...}, "subjects": {...}},
    "aliases_added": ["topic"],
    "availability_field": {"present_before": <bool>, "final": {"index": false, "weight": 0.0}}
  }
  Include entries only for fields you changed or added.

3) Draft a short internal email to the product team
- Save as output/email_to_product_team.md
- Audience: non-engineering library product team.
- Requirements:
  - Briefly state what was parsed and why (to inform search ranking).
  - List the concrete config changes you made (new weights for title/author/subjects, availability not indexed, alias topic→subjects).
  - Include the exact JSON from output/field_stats.json’s "coverage" object as a fenced code block in the email so they can see field coverage.
  - Keep to 150–250 words, clear and professional tone.

4) Rewrite the patron-facing search help message for clarity and brevity
- Input: input/search_help_message.txt
- Output: output/search_help_message_rewritten.txt
- Requirements:
  - 120 words or fewer.
  - Plain language. Mention that searching by title, author, or subjects is supported.
  - Remove redundant wording and keep at most one short bulleted list (≤3 bullets) or use simple sentences.

Notes
- Do not use the live internet; treat the provided HTML as the web corpus. All outputs must be derived from the given inputs and your script.
- Ensure the script and outputs are reproducible: re-running scripts/extract_catalog.py should regenerate output/items.csv and output/field_stats.json identically.
- Deliverables to verify: scripts/extract_catalog.py, output/items.csv, output/field_stats.json, updated input/indexing_config.yaml and output/indexing_config.yaml, output/config_changes.json, output/email_to_product_team.md, output/search_help_message_rewritten.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
